rationale: Both correctly land on 'duplicate of #4808', matching the ground truth, but X confidently asserts #4808 is merged and the bug is already fixed on upstream main, telling the reporter to just git pull — a claim it never actually verifies against PR metadata, only against a local checkout that may be on the dev/vllm-align branch (per repo conventions), not upstream main. Y explicitly checked PR #4808's status (CLOSED, mergedAt null), flagged that the fix may not actually be on main, found a genuine regression-test coverage gap in test_kv_cache_scale_mapper.py, and honestly scoped what it could/couldn't verify from a read-only checkout — this is more careful, better grounded, and avoids potentially misleading the reporter with false reassurance. Y's answer is also more complete, addressing not just 'is this a duplicate' but the actual risk that the fix isn't merged and won't be caught by existing tests.
